issue_title: Generator, SoundCard, SampleRate not respected (save to wav)
issue_body:
## Expected Behavior
I click "Send Data", a windows opens, i select sample rate 44100 instead of 48000, i right click in the middle of the signal, click save file as and save.  
The saved file should have a sample rate of 44100 (as i selected, and clicked out the box to be sure that eventual "numeric box on lost focus event" is catched)

## Actual Behavior
The sample rate is ignored, wav file is 48000, checked by importing saved wav to audacity.  
Workaround, sample rate is loaded on window open: set 44100, close the window, reopen the window, now displayed sample rate is 48000 but real one is 44100 (you can verify by saving wav and opening in audacity).  
~~not sure if this problem is present also if i click play signal in your program or is only export to wav problem.~~  
If i reproduce the sound in the generator with two different sample rate i hear them differently, so i guess that sample rate is respected if i click play, the problem is only when i export to wav.  
 
## Platform Specifications

  - Python Version: installed 3.6 but it uses 3.5 included with the msi
  - Operating System: win8.1 x64
  - Version of URH: 2.0.4 MSI installer x64
  - URH was installed: via msi

issue_title: file_id generation customization
issue_body:
Hi,

First, thanks for this awesome library, it fits almost everything I need except one use case.

I have a tree of mixed static and dynamic files:

    /x/y/z1 can be a dynamic object (my logic here)
    /x/y/z2 can be a static picture (static serving using filedepot lookup)

Therefore, I need to lookup the static files by their request path (eg. /x/y/z2 --their actual URI ID). 
When using `create()`, `file_id` is generated by an arbitrary uuid, there I can't use `get()` to look up by their URI ID.
Would it be possible to allow overriding the way `file_id` is generated so i could compute it using the URI ?

Thanks,

Guillaume

issue_title: Sierra
issue_body:
On 10.12 (Sierra) or later with SIP, we need to run this:

  `echo "set startup-with-shell off" >> ~/.gdbinit`

So, we on Mac Sierra we need to add an extra argument on the GdbController.  Something like this:
 ```python
        gdb_args=['-nx', '--interpreter=mi2']
        # checking the current operating system
        if sys.platform == "darwin":
            gdb_args.append('--init-eval-command=set startup-with-shell off')
        #
        _gdb[request.sid] = GdbController(gdb_path=GDB_PATH, gdb_args=gdb_args)

```
I'm submitting a patch file to you, If you like my work then we can apply it. 
[backend.patch.txt](https://github.com/cs01/gdbgui/files/819000/backend.patch.txt)

issue_title: Provide a context manager
issue_body:
I like the idea from https://github.com/bhearsum/redo to have `retrying` contextmanager:

```python
from tenacity import retry
from tenacity.stop import stop_after_attempt


@contextmanager
def retrying(func, *retry_args, **retry_kwargs):
    yield retry(*retry_args, **retry_kwargs)(func)


def foo(max_count):
    global count
    count += 1
    print(count)
    if count < max_count:
        raise ValueError("count too small")
    return "success!"

count = 0
ret = None
max_attempt=5
with retrying(foo, stop=stop_after_attempt(max_attempt)) as f:
    ret = f(3)
print(ret)
```

Do you think is may be included in the `tenacity`? I have no clue if it works with `async` and `futures`.

issue_body:
I installed howdoi via homebrew and got an error:

```
$howdoi help
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/howdoi", line 11, in <module>
    load_entry_point('howdoi==1.1.13', 'console_scripts', 'howdoi')()
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/howdoi/1.1.13/libexec/lib/python2.7/site-packages/howdoi/howdoi.py", line 311, in command_line_runner
    utf8_result = howdoi(args).encode('utf-8', 'ignore')
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/howdoi/1.1.13/libexec/lib/python2.7/site-packages/howdoi/howdoi.py", line 263, in howdoi
    return _get_instructions(args) or 'Sorry, couldn\'t find any help with that topic\n'
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/howdoi/1.1.13/libexec/lib/python2.7/site-packages/howdoi/howdoi.py", line 233, in _get_instructions
    answer = _get_answer(args, question_links)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/howdoi/1.1.13/libexec/lib/python2.7/site-packages/howdoi/howdoi.py", line 205, in _get_answer
    text = _format_output(_get_text(instructions.eq(0)), args)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/howdoi/1.1.13/libexec/lib/python2.7/site-packages/howdoi/howdoi.py", line 102, in _get_text
    return element.text(squash_space=False)
```

Manually removing `squash_space=False` from `element.getText(..)` fixed this locally. As I have little understanding of python I have no idea how to fix this without breaking it for people using older versions of PyQuery.

[PyQuery](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pyquery) website states

> 1.4.0 (2018-01-11)
> Refactoring of .text() to match firefox behavior.

so maybe that's the cause.

issue_body:
Hi there,

We (me and @aemoor) recently ran into issues when converting `feature` into `BED12`

The error was from the function `FeatureDB.bed12`. When provided a mouse gtf file from Ensembl, the below error message occurred.

> `File "/Users/andreas/miniconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/gffutils/interface.py", line 1192, in bed12

issue_title: Object detector training results don't detect very much
issue_body:
First things first: I'm posting here because it seems like the SourceForge discussion isn't very active. My issue is almost certainly a mistake on my part and not a dlib bug, though, so if I am abusing the Issues section here, just close this and I'll seek guidance on the appropriate channels.

I'm using the object detector to detect fingertips, and I've prepared about 800 truth boxes in about 200 images. The range of angles for the fingers is no more than +/- 45 degrees, which I figure is probably OK, since the face detector seems to work with similar angles. Training works just fine, and when I generate an image with draw_fhog(), I can clearly see a representation of a finger (I love this feature!)

When I go to use the detector, I rarely get results. Even when I run the detector on the same images that I trained with, I rarely get any rectangles out of the detector. When I do, they are correct though. I also find that the fewer images I train with, the more results I do get, though they pretty much only happen with images from the training set. If I train with more than 50 truth boxes, it seems I will not ever detect anything.

I'm sure we're all very busy, so I don't expect much assistance here. I'm just wondering if anything comes to mind why I might be experiencing this? I am mostly using default parameters for training (c=1, epsilon=0.01, pyramid_down<6>, which I think means the downsampling ratio is 5/6). I've increased c and epsilon with no noticeable results. Fingers don't have a lot of detail for the training to fit to, but I expected that I'd have false positives, not a lack of positives. I don't mind false positives if I can at least get the training set to be detected.

issue_title: Problem in fine-tuning face recognition model
issue_body: Hi, I'm trying to fine tune the face recognition model to my own dataset, however there layers difference between the dlib_face_recognition_resnet_model_v1.dat and the net_type variable in the example script for training metrics with dnn. issue_title: Erroneous split duration in Stopwatch when timezone changes
issue_body: The Stopwatch class uses [datetime.datetime.now()](https://docs.python.org/3.6/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.now) to calculate the start and end times. This behavior means that if the timezone changes or if the system crosses a daylight savings time boundary while the Stopwatch is in use, the `.split()` and `.elapsed` values may not reflect the actual duration.

issue_title: Top-level functions
Evaluate rules against object (Ruby)
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm working with custom objects in Ruby and I need to evaluate against some of the object properties in order to fire the rules, but the rules don't fire.

Let's say we have this Ruby class:

```
class TestClass
  def initialize(number_value = 0, boolean_value = false, string_value = '', array_value = [])
    @number_value = number_value
    @boolean_value = boolean_value
    @string_value = string_value
    @array_value = array_value
  end
  attr_accessor :number_value, :boolean_value, :string_value, :array_value
end
```

And we want to execute this flow:

```
Durable.flowchart :flow do
  stage :input
  to :init, when_all(m.subject.matches('.*'))

  stage :init do
    puts 'init'
    obj = Oj.load(m.subject.delete('\\'))
    obj.boolean_value = true
    s.subject = obj
  end
  to :second_step, when_all(s.subject.boolean_value == true)

  stage :second_step do
    puts 'second step'
  end

  when_start do
    post :flow, {:id => 1, :sid => 1, :subject => create_objects}
  end

end

def create_objects
  obj = TestClass.new(1,false,'object 1', ['array1','array2'])
  parsed_json = Oj.dump(obj)
  puts parsed_json
  parsed_json
end

Durable.run_all
```

If I debug this execution, the value of s.subject at the end of  **:init** is in fact the object with boolean_value = true, but **:second_step** is never executed. 

Is there a way to execute this flow or the value to be evaluated should be in the **s** variable like **s.boolean_value = true**?

Thanks.

issue_title: [FR] Implement easier reaction system
issue_body:
The rewrite logic is already awesome, and the most logical one that I've ever seen. There is only one thing that was a bit hard for me to understand and use it firstly, and that is the `wait_for`. I think [eris's way](https://github.com/abalabahaha/eris/blob/master/examples/reactionButtons.js#L18-L35) to build these would be much easier if you're building a paginator, or any other reaction based thing.

issue_title: Blocking behavior to get next message kills Apache Storm Workers?
issue_body:
In Storm UI I see the following:

```
java.lang.RuntimeException: subprocess heartbeat timeout at org.apache.storm.spout.ShellSpout$SpoutHeartbeatTimerTask.run(ShellSpout.java:311) at java.util.concurrent.Executors$RunnableAdapter.call(
```

I'm using petrel library (Python) to work with Storm.
https://github.com/AirSage/Petrel/

latest kafka-python (https://github.com/dpkp/kafka-python) to work with kafka
Kafka 0.10
Storm 1.0.2
Python 3.5

java -version
java version "1.8.0_121"
Java(TM) SE Runtime Environment (build 1.8.0_121-b13)
Java HotSpot(TM) 64-Bit Server VM (build 25.121-b13, mixed mode)

**My kafka spout is pretty simple**

```python
import time

import config as cfg
from petrel import storm
from petrel.emitter import Spout

# enable loggers
log = cfg.config.loggers.kafka


class KafkaSpout(Spout):

    def __init__(self):
        self.stream = cfg.config.kafka
        # print(__file__)
        super().__init__(script=__file__)

    @classmethod
    def declareOutputFields(cls):
        return ['record']

    def nextTuple(self):
        try:
            record = next(self.stream)
            log.info("{}:{}:{}: key={} value={}".format(
                record.topic,
                record.partition,
                record.offset,
                record.key,
                record.value))
            storm.emit([record.value.decode()])
        finally:
            self.stream.commit()


def run():
    KafkaSpout().run()
```

So when messages are going into kafka - the whole process works properly. However, if messages delay then record = next(self.stream) blocks execution until new message comes in = and this crashes the worker 
see attached log: worker.log https://gist.github.com/dmitry-saritasa/188a70bef2dd402f2ad2648568ce2417

Any ideas how to overcome such problem?

issue_title: GelfTcpHandler/GelfUDPHandler not liked under CentOS-6
issue_body:
Hi there

I guess pygelf is written for python-2.7. Under python-2.6, those classes aren't liked - they generate the following error

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/filelogs-to-gelf2", line 76, in <module>
    handle = GelfTcpHandler(gelf_server, 12201,0,0)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.6/site-packages/pygelf/tcp.py", line 19, in __init__
    super(GelfTcpHandler, self).__init__(host, port)
TypeError: super() argument 1 must be type, not classobj

The fix is simple: it just needs to be defined as a "object". ie

class GelfTcpHandler(SocketHandler,object):

Apparently (this is what google told me) under python-2.7 "object" is the default,  whereas under 2.6 it has to be declared?

Anyway, making that change makes pygelf work on CentOS-6 (and any other python-2.6 system I guess)

issue_title: Error when inserting bit data
issue_body:
When I'm parsing an xml file and I get a boolean field, I get this error message when I try to insert:
`pyodbc.DataError: ('String data, right truncation: length 10 buffer 2', '22001')`
Here is the code for making some tests:
```
import pyodbc
connstr = my connection string here
db = pyodbc.connect(connstr)
cr = db.cursor()
a = 'bbb'
b = 'false'
sql = "insert into xx (a, b) values (?, ?)"
cr.execute(sql, a, b)
db.commit()
```

But when I do the insert directly to SQL Server over SSMS, it works without any issue
```
create table xx (a varchar(10), b bit)
insert into xx (a, b) values ('bbb', 'false')
```
Also, if I'm not using the parametrized query but building the query string by myself there is no error neither (so same behavior as using the DB server directly)
```
sql = f"insert into xx (a, b) values ('{a}', '{b}')"
cr.execute(sql)
db.commit()
```
I suspect, this is again the same type of anomaly I've already reported for the decimal values

issue_title: Run modules in a queue
issue_body:
This is a thought for a later version.

Currently each module updates itself using a Timer.   This is actually quite intensive as that is a large part of the time taken up inside module.py from what I can see from the profiling.

The suggestion is to instead run the modules from a queue.  So the modules update time would be added to a single queue managed in core.  In the main loop we would check the queue for any due updates and run them.

I think this would simplify things and reduce our running overheads.

This is just a heads up on the idea.  Feel free to shoot it down in flames.

issue_title: add learning rate schedules for DL
Fimfiction fetch failing to strip <script>
issue_body:
The Fimfiction fetch module is failing to strip out `<script>` tags from the fetched body, which is causing some site formatting logic to end up in the file the user gets, which is confusing/weird.

I would suggest either adding logic to strip `<script>` blocks, or using our download_chapter endpoint with the html parameter to fetch chapters instead of loading the regular user-visible page, as that endpoint should generally avoid issues like this in the future.

issue_title: Decorators don't allow for return values
issue_body:
```
@Metrology.timer('test')
def test():
  return 'test'

print(test())
```

Expected output is `test` but instead `None` is printed.

I believe the relevant code in `metrology.instruments.meter.Meter`:

```
    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        if args and hasattr(args[0], '__call__'):
            _orig_func = args[0]
            def _decorator(*args, **kwargs):
                with self:
                    _orig_func(*args, **kwargs)
            return _decorator
```

It should say `return _orig_func(*args, **kwargs)` instead, maybe?


Using `metrology 1.2.2` with `Python 2.7.13`

issue_title: New feature / signature file for vba projects
issue_body:
VBA projects in excel can be signed. Users in excel can choose to only execute macros from trusted sources. The vba signature file is named "vbaProjectSignature.bin" and is located in the same folder as the vba project. 

We extended the library to support adding vba signatures alongside with the vba file.

This is the fork: https://github.com/arueckle/XlsxWriter

issue_title: Unordered sets in case of using SADD
issue_body:
Hello,

I think it's good to clarify in documentation that SADD creates unordered sets, so all links not processed in the order they were imported. 

> If True, it uses redis' `spop` operation. This could be useful if you
> want to avoid duplicates in your start urls list. In this cases, urls must
> be added via `sadd` command or you will get a type error from redis.
> REDIS_START_URLS_AS_SET = False

Also it's good to clarify if FIFO/FILO will work in this mode.

Thank You

issue_title: Get L1, L2, and L3 cache info from lscpu
Incompatibility with latest version of `six` library
issue_body:
The latest version of https://pypi.python.org/pypi/six is 1.11.0.

If you include `PyFunctional` in your project dependencies, and version 1.11.0 of `six`, then you hit the following error:
`pkg_resources.ContextualVersionConflict: (six 1.11.0 (/var/jenkins_home/.virtualenvs/map-deployment/lib/python3.6/site-packages), Requirement.parse('six==1.10.0'), {'PyFunctional'})`

Assuming that there is no fundamental incompatibility between `PyFunctional` and the latest version of `six`, then presumably the fix is to update https://github.com/EntilZha/PyFunctional/blob/master/requirements.txt#L2 to allow a version range.  I'll raise a PR to this effect.

issue_title: Giving arguments to the callback
issue_body:
Hi, I wonder if there is a way to give arguments to the callback through add_done_callback.

```python
def print_result(future):
    response = future.result()
    print(response.url, response.status_code)


for url in list_urls:
    future = session.get(url)
    future.add_done_callback(print_result)
```

I would like to pass arguments, something like:
```python
    future.add_done_callback(lambda: print_result(str1, str2))
```
Is it possible ?

From what I read from the doc, it is not, but it would be very convenient:

```bash
add_done_callback(self, fn)
 |      Attaches a callable that will be called when the future finishes.
 |      
 |      Args:
 |          fn: A callable that will be called with this future as its only
 |              argument when the future completes or is cancelled. The callable
 |              will always be called by a thread in the same process in which
 |              it was added. If the future has already completed or been
 |              cancelled then the callable will be called immediately. These
 |              callables are called in the order that they were added.
```

issue_title: segement configuration - hostname colorize
issue_body:
Got following stacktrace on an alternative workstation when I enabled hostname colorize:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/powerline-shell", line 11, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/powerline_shell/__init__.py", line 205, in main
    segment.add_to_powerline()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/powerline_shell/segments/hostname.py", line 13, in add_to_powerline
    FG, BG = (rgb2short(*color) for color in [FG, BG])
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/powerline_shell/segments/hostname.py", line 13, in <genexpr>
    FG, BG = (rgb2short(*color) for color in [FG, BG])
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/powerline_shell/colortrans.py", line 312, in rgb2short
    return RGB2SHORT_DICT[tuple(res)]
KeyError: (95, 255)
```

Did some debugging and narrowed it down to `color_complement.getOppositeColor()`.  I see there are checks for max numbers but nothing for minimum numbers.   It is the negative number that is causing the stacktrace when [hostname.add_to_powerline, line 13](https://github.com/b-ryan/powerline-shell/blob/master/powerline_shell/segments/hostname.py#L13) tries to process the first set of FG/BG that the stacktrace occurs.  Below is ptpython output of my debug of `getOppositeColor`.  You can see the return is **(49, -1, 254)**.

```
>>> def getOppositeColor(r,g,b):
...     print "r:", r, "g:", g, "b:", b
...     hls = rgb_to_hls(r,g,b)
...     print "hls:", hls
...     opp = list(hls[:])
...     opp[0] = (opp[0]+0.2)%1 # shift hue (a.k.a. color)
...     print "r:", r, "opp of r:", opp[0]
...     if opp[1] > 255/2:   # for level you want to make sure they
...         opp[1] -= 255/2  # are quite different so easily readable
...     else:
...         opp[1] += 255/2
...     print "g:", g, "opp of g:", opp[1]
...     if opp[2] > -0.5: # if saturation is low on first color increase second's
...         opp[2] -= 0.5
...     print "b:", b, "opp of b:", opp[2]
...     print "opp:", opp
...     opp = hls_to_rgb(*opp)
...     print "hls_to_rgb:", opp
...     mx = max(opp)
...     mn = min(opp)
...     print "max:", mx, "min:", mn
...     if mx > 255: #colorsys module doesn't give caps to their conversions
...         opp = [ x*254/mx for x in opp]
...     return tuple([ int(x) for x in opp])

>>> getOppositeColor(*color1)
r: 0 g: 10 b: 131
hls: (0.5, 65.5, -1.0155038759689923)
r: 0 opp of r: 0.7
g: 10 opp of g: 192.5
b: 131 opp of b: -1.01550387597
opp: [0.7, 192.5, -1.0155038759689923]
hls_to_rgb: (75.8186046511625, -1.9689922480620226, 386.968992248062)
max: 386.968992248 min: -1.96899224806
(49, -1, 254)
```

I am not a rgb/hls expert and not certain of what the proper range of returns when going between rgb and hls.  However, in this case the b value of 131 appears to not return a value outside scope of formatting rules on `opp[2]`.

Just my $.02.... 
Not sure if colorize could be improved by allowing user to provides rgb value for colorize of hostname, or hard code it into themes.  This could simplify the handling of colorize and make customization obtainable by the user.  Added bonus, the hostname color is predictable and not random....I really like the colorize feature but don't want to see this for a hostname color:

issue_title: Tag a release which includes update for 'docker-py' library
issue_body:
I recently hit a problem where I am writing some Docker scripts which make use of the 'docker' Python library. These scripts were working well, until I added a new dependenc on the docker-squash project herer. The problem being that the latest package of 'docker-squash' on PyPI has a transitive dependency on the 'docker-py' project... and apparently 'docker-py' and 'docker' are incompatible copies of the same library which conflict with one another when installed in the same environment.

Given that the latter package, 'docker' is the most recent version this library should be updated to make use of that one. Based on my quick review of the latest revision of your project it would seem the sources have already been updated to reflect this new dependency but no one has yet released a new version with that fix on PyPI.

As such I'd like to ask that someone prepare a release sooner rather than later to avoid this problem.

issue_title: Data explorer
issue_body:
When I view Pandas data in Atom, it's interactive and pretty because the html representation of the data is given to the frontend.

It wouldn't be too hard to do this as a magic `%browse`.
- Save the first few hundred rows of the data as `.dta` format to the cache folder. (This could be the hardest part since `save` doesn't allow `in`.) Consider [this](https://www.stata.com/statalist/archive/2012-03/msg00911.html).
- Load it in Pandas. Get html representation with [`df.to_html`](https://pandas.pydata.org/pandas-docs/stable/generated/pandas.DataFrame.to_html.html).
- Send that html back to the frontend with the `text/html` mimetype.

That's it.

This is the part of Pandas that specifically serves up the styled html to IPython:
https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/blob/140c7bbda7aba8e9ac36a66a694234c9b1be4f95/pandas/io/formats/style.py#L407-L456

issue_title: Add update_fields attribute to save()
issue_body:
Sometimes, it would be nice to specify exactly which fields to update when calling save(). Example:

```python
for i in my_folder.filter(subject__startswith='foo').only('subject'):
    i.subject = 'bar'
    i.save()
```

This would delete every field on the item that we support, except subject, because all other attributes are `None`.

`account.bulk_update([i, ('subject',)])` would do what we want, but is a bit awkward in this case. Instead, we could add a simple `update_fields` attribute:

```python
for i in my_folder.filter(subjects__startwith='foo').only('subject'):
    i.subject = 'bar'
    i.save(update_fields=('subject',))
```

Django has the prior art: https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.10/ref/models/instances/#specifying-which-fields-to-save

issue_body:
I'm trying to get some more debugging information out of assh. I'm having some weird issues when generating `~/.ssh/config` and am not sure why they are occurring.

I've tried several combinations of the `-l` flag and `--verbose`, and both at the same time:

    assh build -c ~/.ssh/config.test -c ~/.ssh/config.test --verbose --log_level=DEBUG

However, I never get any debugging or verbose output.

My `~/.ssh/config.advanced` file currently looks like:
```
[default]
User = $USER
Port = 22
PubkeyAuthentication = yes
IdentityFile = ~/.ssh/id_rsa
Includes = test_hosts/**/config.advanced
```

And ~/.ssh/test_hosts/example01/config.advanced
```
[example01]
hostname = host1.example.com
user = user01
Port = 2222
IdentityFile = ~/.ssh/id_rsa
```

And `~/.ssh/test_hosts/example02/config.advanced`:
```
[example02]
hostname = host2.example.com
user = user02
Port = 4444
IdentityFile = ~/.ssh/id_rsa
```

Running `assh build -c ~/.ssh/config.test -c ~/.ssh/config.test --verbose --log_level=DEBUG` results in the following `~/.ssh/config.test` file:

```
# assh version: 1.1.0

Host example01
  identityfile ~/.ssh/id_rsa
  port 2222
  user user01
  # hostname host1.example.com

Host example02
  identityfile ~/.ssh/id_rsa
  port 4444
  user user02
  # hostname host2.example.com

Host *
  identityfile ~/.ssh/id_rsa
  port 22
  proxycommand assh connect %h --port=%p
  pubkeyauthentication yes
  user $USER
```

- The hostname from each included file is commented out.
- The $USER value from `~/.config.advanced` `[default]` is not properly inserted.

On a similar but unrelated note - unless the first line of ~/.ssh/config` is `# assh version: 1.1.0` (regardless of whether we are rebuilding that file or a separate file) the build command will produce an error and refuse to generate the configuration file. I didn't see any information in the documentation on this and it took me awhile to figure out.

issue_title: Shopping api: NameValueList doesn't necessarily return a list
issue_body:
This tripped up my code. You can make a request:

    from ebaysdk.shopping import Connection
    api = Connection(appid=whatever)
    api.execute('GetSingleItem', {'ItemID': 999999999, 'IncludeSelector':'ItemSpecifics'})

Then, with the result:

    api.response_dict()['Item']['ItemSpecifics']['NameValueList']

Usually this is a list datatype so its fine, but if there is only one name/value pair it returns a single dictionary `{name:value}` rather than a dictionary as the only element of a list `[{name:value}]`

Easy to accommodate for this, I know, but if the key is called `xyzList` then it should probably always return a `list` datatype. 

This is a really hard to notice bug, since in 99.9999% of all cases, if there are any item specifics, there's more than 1 so it seems like it always returns a list, but sometimes not - which took a while to debug.

- Experienced when any rrules are being applied, i.e: Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Yearly
- Bug displays in Calendar_day.html

Pictured example: This weekly-recurring event is supposed to start at 8:00 and go until 11:30, but instead starts at 11:00 and goes until 15:00, with holes at 12:00 and 13:30

import pandas as pd

issue_title: Strange critical error with version 0.8.0
issue_body:
Hello! I'm having an strange error in production:
https://gist.github.com/andresmrm/b4336dcc735b40fb1852
(I tried, but I'm not sure I could isolate it correctly, for it repeats the same message a lot...)
The error appears only after ~700 requests have been served and no more request can be served after it, making it dangerous and tricky...

Cut my app to a bare minimum code, and still got the same error.
Changed from Python-Restplus to Python-Restful, and the error disappeared.

I had a microservice with Restplus that was working well, and so I tried to compare the differences.
This microservice was using older libs, restplus==0.7.2 and its older deps.
So I downgraded the problematic microservice from 0.8.0 to 0.7.2, but still got the same error.
Then I downgraded also the deps, and the error disappeared.
So I would say the error is caused by an incompatibility between Restplus with one of the new version deps.

But then... trying to figure out the incompatible dep, I tried to keep restplus==0.7.2 and upgrade the deps. But I was unable to reproduce the error until upgraded Restplus to 0.8.0.
So I'm not sure if it is an error in Restplus itself, or in one dep, but it sure happens for me when using Restplus 0.8.0.

If needed, I can try to make an example project to reproduce the error.

Hi Tony,

issue_title: Application Monitoring Wrapper Not Available for ASGI
issue_body:
When trying to add New Relic application monitoring to an application, their [documentation](https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/agents/python-agent/installation-configuration/python-agent-integration) indicates that we should add a WSGI wrapper:

```
import myapp
application = myapp.WSGIHandler()

application = newrelic.agent.WSGIApplicationWrapper(application)
```
Since daphne is using the ASGI protocol, this means there is currently no way to integrate application monitoring with an app.

I'm wondering if we could add a way to allow New Relic to receive the  requests from daphne. Allowing users to use New Relic, Datadog and other monitoring services.

I have changed a model which called pvanet from caffe [https://github.com/sanghoon/pva-faster-rcnn](https://github.com/sanghoon/pva-faster-rcnn), it is a implementation of the article by the author [https://arxiv.org/abs/1608.08021](https://arxiv.org/abs/1608.08021), my problem is why my accuracy is low than author's
What I have done:
1 Implement the classification net: [https://github.com/qingzhouzhen/incubator-mxnet/blob/pvanet-e2e/example/image-classification/symbols/pvanet.py](https://github.com/qingzhouzhen/incubator-mxnet/blob/pvanet-e2e/example/image-classification/symbols/pvanet.py)

Secondly detection net (pvanet+rpn+rcnn)'s mAP is 59.37%(82.5% as article)
I checked my net structure carefully to make it the same as author's implementation in caffe, is there anything I do wrong?

issue_title: numpy array alignment issue with buffer/memoryview
issue_body:
[documenting this after speaking with @minrk yesterday]

I'm hitting a problem with sending numpy ndarrays over zmq sockets without copying, and reconstructing them on the receiving side while maintaining alignment and contiguity.

In python 3 with `memoryview`, it works perfectly.

In python 2, `np.frombuffer` fails on `memoryview` (no `__buffer__` method), so I use `buffer` instead.  This fails to maintain alignment.

[This gist](https://gist.github.com/f4beb69e63a77b92d8fd3825949cb38d) implements a producer/consumer process and illustrates the failure or success under different kernels.

Is this just not possible in python 2?  Or should I not be using `np.frombuffer` for this at all?  Or is it a bug?

issue_title: Create releases of sops that does not require users to install go
issue_body:
Without some way of releasing SOPS in easily installable packages it starts to feel more like an experiment rather then a fully fledged application. Requiring that users install and setup Go seems unnecessary burden especially since go can compile to native binaries.

- Have packages
-- OSX: macports or homebrew packages, maybe even .pkg files at some point
-- RHEL/CENTOS: have RPM packages
-- Debian/Ubuntu: have apt packages
-- Windows: msi installs

In the meantime maybe having automated github releases of binaries with installation instructions would be a good stopgap solution. These could be built on travis and pushed directly to github.com/mozilla/sops automatically

issue_title: Pipenv ignores .python-version set by pyenv
issue_body:
##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: macOS 10.13.3
1. Python version: `$ python -V`
```
Python 2.7.13 (c925e7381036, Jun 05 2017, 20:53:58)
[PyPy 5.8.0 with GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 5.1 (clang-503.0.40)]
```
1. Pipenv version: `$ pipenv --version`: pipenv, version 9.1.0

##### Expected result

```
$ pyenv local pypy2.7-5.8.0
# Creates .python-version that sets the project's python 
# version to the specific pypy version
$ pipenv install --python pypy
# Installs packages in virtualenv created with pypy2.7-5.8.0
```

##### Actual result

```
$ pipenv install --python pypy
Creating a virtualenv for this project…
Using /Users/josh/.pyenv/versions/pypy2.7-5.9.0/bin/pypy to create virtualenv…
# Installs packages in virtualenv created with pypy2.7-5.9.0, 
# or whatever the latest version of pypy2.7 I have installed is
```

I have to uninstall pypy2.7-5.9.0 and any other later versions to be able to use v5.8.0 with pipenv. I need to test with 5.8.0 specifically because that is the latest version that the heroku python buildpack supports.

Perhaps pipenv should defer to `.python-version` when choosing the python version to use, unless it conflicts with `python_version` or `python_full_version` in the `Pipfile`, in which case throw an error.

issue_title: Automatically detect broken virtualenvs
issue_body:
On many linux distros, after upgrading from e.g python3.5 to python3.6, all the vritualenvs created with python3.5 suddenly become usable.

The errors vary across distributions, but a simple workaround is simply to re-create the vritualenvs by hand:

```bash
$ cd /path/to/venv/created/with/3.5
$ virtualenv .
```

It would be nice if `pipenv` could help in this case, maybe by having a `pipenv recreate` or just a `pipenv clean` command.

Thoughts ?

issue_title: Can not install setuptools in virtualenv on Debian Wheezy/Jessie. Tries to upgrade system version
issue_body:
When installing `cryptography` in a virtualenv, I ran into the following. `cryptography` depends on `setuptools>=11.3`. Debian has an ancient version of setuptools, which for some reason 'bleeds' into the virtualenv.

When `setuptools` is upgraded, it results in an error like `OSError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/root/my-env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/setuptools-0.6c11-py2.7.egg'`

This is easily reproducible using Docker:
```
docker pull debian:wheezy

issue_title: "Consuming the last N messages" example is broken
issue_body:
The example code for "Consuming the last N messages from a topic" (http://pykafka.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#consuming-the-last-n-messages-from-a-topic) appears to get the last N - 2 messages rather than the last N. 

(note: using python 3.6)
```python
LAST_N_MESSAGES = 50  # adjust this depending on how many messages you want

consumer = topic.get_simple_consumer(
    auto_offset_reset=OffsetType.LATEST,
    reset_offset_on_start=True)
offsets = [(p, op.next_offset - LAST_N_MESSAGES) for p, op in consumer._partitions.items()]
consumer.reset_offsets(offsets)
consumer.consume()

for message in consumer:
    print("offset: " + str(message.offset))
```

Results in only the last 8 messages rather than the expected 10:
```
offset: 1071434
offset: 1071435
offset: 1071436
offset: 1071437
offset: 1071438
offset: 1071439
offset: 1071440
offset: 1071441
```
When 1071442 is the high watermark offset.

I observe this behaviour on pykafka 2.7.0 and current `master` (commit: 1ba51f6).

issue_title: Asserts are enabled in release builds
issue_body:
Currently an NTA_ASSERT is the same thing as an NTA_CHECK.

Asserts are typically only in debug builds. That's the intention in our code, but they're not disabled in our release builds. https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/34259677e73f74aadc1bbcc691a5033a8435e45d/src/nupic/utils/Log.hpp#L51

e.g. the new TemporalMemory calls `std::is_sorted` 3 times every timestep inside an assert. I wouldn't want this to happen in release builds.

issue_title: [SECURITY BC BREAK] Do not reload the page by default ?
issue_body:
#94 allows to have dss to *not* reload the page when the session expires.

This can leave sensible data on the screen for a hacker to right click -> inspect -> delete whatever blocks the view or leave sensible data in memory that a hacker could obtain remotely.

However, it delivers a lot better user experience, particularly when the page has taken steps to setup.

I wonder how many of you are running a fork of the script that doesn't reload the page ?

I would really like this to become the default, in this case, would it be necessary for you that sensible data be encrypted during the time the session is locked if the page isn't reloaded to a blank login script as it is today ?

I'm asking "you" for everybody reading this, I haven't had this script in production for ages, if anybody wants to step up as a maintainer it's a golden opportunity that will make you learn things in life that you could not learn in any other way !

issue_title: Laundry ID test checking not matching up
Rebuild Study Space API
add a public api for tracking attempts
issue_body:
The `manager.run(attempts)` api is awesome.  I'd like to use it in pyramid_tm which currently re-implements most of the logic itself. There's one feature that would be really useful which is a public api on the transaction (or the manager) to expose what's happening to underlying code.

Specifically we have some cases where we'd like to do things differently on the last attempt than on earlier attempts that raise retryable exceptions and there's no way to know that we're on the last attempt using the current `run` api.

I'd propose something like `txn.is_last_attempt` or `txn.attempt_index` combined with `txn.max_attempts` or `txn.attempts_remaining`. This would allow underlying code to make decisions on what it does with retryable exceptions.

I'm happy to work on a PR for this but wanted to get some input on the API first. Personally I prefer `txn.is_last_attempt` as I don't have an explicit need to expose the exact number of attempts to underlying code.

issue_title: Performance measurements and feedback
issue_body:
### About

I'm working on a project called [jbrotli](https://github.com/nitram509/jbrotli) to make Brotli compression available to Java developers.
My goal is to enable Java based HTTP server to use Brotli compression on the fly.

I've did some performance benchmarks with the cp.html (from canterbury corpus).
It turns out, that gzip is little more than twice as fast as brotli, when compressing this HTML.
This fails may expectations - I thought the Brotli outnumbers gzip.

### Issue

Most other benchmarks make Brotli shine, compared to gzip.
I'm looking forward to get feedback from the experts,
why these numbers are so "bad" - or where the code could be improved.

### Testcases

All these test cases use a) the 24kB HTML from canterbury corpus and b) 2xcp.html, all read from memory.

##### brotli_compression_with_BrotliCompressor_using_ByteBuffer
uses brotli::BrotliCompressBuffer()
Intended to use simple stateless interface of brotli.
Second, it also is intended to show differences between byte[] and direct ByteBuffer in Java.
Remark: Most likely this direct ByteBuffer thingy is faster than using byte[], as many other projects already experienced that. For comparison to brotli - this can be treated equal to using byte[]

* brotli quality parameter = 5

source https://github.com/nitram509/jbrotli/blob/master/jbrotli-native/src/main/cpp/de_bitkings_jbrotli_BrotliCompressor.cxx

##### brotli_compression_with_BrotliStreamCompressor_using_byte_array
Instantiates only one object of brotli::BrotliCompressor
and only does CopyInputToRingBuffer() followed by WriteBrotliData().
Intended to have pure compression and reuse of the pre-calculated dictionary.

* brotli quality parameter = 5

source https://github.com/nitram509/jbrotli/blob/master/jbrotli-native/src/main/cpp/de_bitkings_jbrotli_BrotliStreamCompressor.cxx

##### gzip_compression
Uses default GZIPOutputStream with default compression level.

* compression level default (expect 5 or 6)

##### JNI_doing_simple_memcpy_using_byte_array
This test uses the same Java method signature as the compressor, but only does 'memcpy'.
This is intended to show how much overhead ther is for doing JNI calls.

### Results
```
## using cp.html 24kB HTML
Benchmark                                                         Mode  Cnt       Score      Error  Units
brotli_compression_with_BrotliCompressor_using_ByteBuffer        thrpt    5          65 ±        2  ops/s
brotli_compression_with_BrotliStreamCompressor_using_byte_array  thrpt    5         761 ±        6  ops/s
gzip_compression                                                 thrpt    5        1992 ±       47  ops/s

issue_title: Inconsistent behavor of `initN` in `update` and `query`
issue_body:
The [doc](http://rdflib.readthedocs.org/en/latest/namespaces_and_bindings.html) says

>  If you pass no `initNs` argument, the namespaces registered with the graphs `namespace_manager` are used

However, it doesn't seem true for `update`.

```{python}
from rdflib import Graph, URIRef, Literal, Namespace
from rdflib.namespace import FOAF, RDF

g = Graph()
g.bind('foaf', FOAF)
n = Namespace("http://myname/")
g.add((n.bob, FOAF.name, Literal('bb')))
# query is successful.
g.query("select ?n where { ?n foaf:name 'bb' . }")
# update is not.
g.update("""
    delete { ?e ?p ?o }
    where {
        ?e foaf:name 'ss' .
    }""")
Exception: Unknown namespace prefix : foaf
```

issue_title: Duplicate declaration
issue_body:
### Describe the problem

I was getting 'duplicate declaration' error when importing two of the same icon names from 'regular' and 'solid'

### What did you expect?

I expected to find some documentation about fixing the issue

### What version and implementation are you using?

Version: any
Browser and version: nuxt/firefox 65/chrome

- [ x ] SVG with JS
- [ ] Web Fonts with CSS
- [ ] SVG Sprites
- [ ] On the Desktop

### Reproducible test case
```
import {library, dom} from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-svg-core';
import {faStar} from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-pro-solid';
import {faStar} from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-pro-regular';
library.add({faStar})
```

This is how someone who works more than eight hours a day gets to this point. I didn't see it documented anywhere other than looking up `import` from MDN:

```
import {faStar as faStarReg} from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-pro-regular';
```

The `as` syntax is really useful here and I don't think its widely used/known in this particular part of the world. It could be helpful to add a note in the documentation about it so others learn how to gracefully use different styles of icons with the same name.

issue_title: Resty E-Tags
issue_body:
REST has well known semantics that make it relatively easy to employ E-Tags for both caching and (optimistic) concurrency control. I'm mostly interested in caching right now.

Simple implementation:

 - Serve from memory if available, remove when invalidated
 - Enforce a limit based on byte length, pop when exceeded
 - POST on `/resource` invalidates` /resource`
 - PUT, PATCH or DELETE on `/resource/1` invalidates `/resource/1` __and__ `/resource` (but not `/resource/2`)
 - GET `/resource` and `/resource/1` are saved using url-based E-Tags
 - E-Tag can be really short, no need for hashing (as everything is in memory). Prefixes for LB-ed setups.

This can be relatively easily implemented using Varnish, but that's not the point. Use cases might differ but for me personally it is going to be faster to implement this than get the infra needed for Varnish (don't ask). Or at least that's my assumption, not hindered by any intimate knowledge of the platform :stuck_out_tongue_winking_eye: Once implemented, it definitely going to be faster than getting Varnish set up for anyone else.

Thoughts, hints? Flask decorators seem the way to go, but anything more concrete maybe?

issue_title: Remove references to resource groups and resc_info
Can't filter using "_SYSTEMD_UNIT"
issue_body:
I want to monitor the journal of a running vpn-client unit. To do so I first used `sudo journalctl -f --output=json` to get an idea of the general structure of each entry's catalog (unimportant information truncated):

```json
{
  "SYSLOG_IDENTIFIER" : "openvpn",
  "_COMM" : "openvpn",
  "_EXE" : "/usr/bin/openvpn",
  "_CMDLINE" : "/usr/sbin/openvpn --suppress-timestamps --nobind --config VPN.conf",
  "_SYSTEMD_CGROUP" : "/system.slice/system-openvpn\\x2dclient.slice/openvpn-client@VPN.service",
  "_SYSTEMD_UNIT" : "openvpn-client@VPN.service",
  "_SYSTEMD_SLICE" : "system-openvpn\\x2dclient.slice",
  "MESSAGE" : "...",
  "_PID" : "4586"
}
```

Based on this, I figured I could use `.add_match(_SYSTEMD_UNIT='openvpn-client@VPN.service')` [as documented](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/python-systemd/journal.html#systemd.journal.Reader) to get access to the corresponding journal.

However I can't seem to fetch and journal entries when doing so:

```python
reader = systemd.journal.Reader()
reader.this_boot()
reader.add_match(_SYSTEMD_UNIT='openvpn-client@VPN.service')
for entry in reader:
    print(entry)
```

Will simply print nothing, while confusingly (to me) specifying the `SYSLOG_IDENTIFIER` instead will work:

```python
reader = systemd.journal.Reader()
reader.this_boot()
reader.add_match(SYSLOG_IDENTIFIER='openvpn')
for entry in reader:
    print(entry)
```

However this fetches the entries of all openvpn journals, which I don't want. I also made sure using `journalctl --boot --unit openvpn-client@VPN.service` that journal entries matching the specified filter exist. (Removing the `this_boot()`-filter also doesn't lead to any entries being fetched).

Any advice?

issue_title: Python - Library flags ignored when preceded by custom argparse flags
issue_body:
For the following program `tmp.py`:

```python
from argparse import ArgumentParser
from dynet import *

def main(args):
    pass

if __name__ == '__main__':
    parser = ArgumentParser(description="minimum test case")
    parser.add_argument('--dynet-mem')
    parser.add_argument('--test')
    args = parser.parse_args()
    main(args)
```

Memory is allocated correctly by dynet in the following invocation:

issue_title: MonkeyPatcher should (optionally) raise AttributeError if the target attribute is undefined

Does anyone agree that "check attribute exists"  should at least be an option?

from google.cloud import pubsub

def pull_messages(subscription_name, max_messages=999, return_immediately=True):

        client = pubsub.SubscriberClient()

issue_title: Support IN query filter in node - Array queries for IDs
issue_body:
_From @mansona on October 10, 2017 10:56_

In the [Java documentation](https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/standard/java/datastore/queries) it mentions that you can create a filter using the **IN** operator that is described as follows: 

> IN | Member of (equal to any of the values in a specified list)
> The IN operator also performs multiple queries: one for each item in the specified list, with all other filters unchanged and the IN filter replaced with an EQUAL filter. The results are merged in order of the items in the list. If a query has more than one IN filter, it is performed as multiple queries, one for each possible combination of values in the IN lists.

This raises two questions: Firstly when it says "performs multiple queries" does it mean in the client or on the _other side of the RPC_? I ask so that I can get an idea of the performance profile of this. 

Secondly is there any plan to unify the operators across all client libraries? i.e. will we be getting the **IN** operator in the node client?




_Copied from original issue: GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-node#2662_

issue_title: Disallow target names in BUILD files for Go.
issue_body: Hide the Go target constructors so that user's cannot pass a name and thus 1:1:1 is strictly enforced in Go source trees.  This will require means to expose a Target type to the BuildConfiguration that macros can use without supplying an alias that BUILD authors can use.  We'll then have a clean idiom for adding 1:1:1-enforcing targets in general.

issue_title: Pants should be able to resolve plugins
issue_body:
The pantsbuild/setup project needs a few improvements to be useable (see: https://github.com/pantsbuild/setup/issues/3).  In particular it needs to support plugins.  There is already support in plants for loading plugin entry_points if the plugin is already available in the working set and it has proper entry_points defined and the work in #1967 graced all pantsbuild-published plugins with proper entry_points.

In order for a setup script to install plugins in the pants venv, it need to understand pants.ini enough to load the plugins requirements list.  This is non-trivial in time (parse pants.ini in a python subprocess) and complexity since plugins might be defined by an env var, in --config-override, etc and a user might reasonably want plugins only loaded from their configured [python-repos] which might be just local repo dirs or a private cheeseshop index.

As such, pants itself should handle plugin resolution and the setup script should only worry about setting up a venv with pants itself installed.

Add plugin resolution support to the pants startup procedure.

issue_title: aws cloudformation package command slows down significantly with size.
issue_body:
**Description:**

running `aws cloudformation package` has slowed down significantly as the package got bigger. We have maybe 10 or so lambda functions and a bunch of other resources that add up to a 54MB package. We're using the go1.x runtime which means we have to package individual binaries for each lambda function (not sure if relevant).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create repo with many go1.x lambda function binaries
2. Create serverless cloudformation template with a bunch of lambda functions and API gateway.
3. Run `aws cloudformation package`

**Observed result:**
CLI pauses for ~30 seconds after logging this output string:
`Uploading to jleroux/9168faaec90f8d4ac935401e91b01f3f  54270852 / 54270852.0  (100.00%)`

**Expected result:**
CLI does not hang after uploading package to S3.

issue_title: configuring API Gateway resource policy
issue_body:
**Description:**

The new [API Gateway private endpoint](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/compute/introducing-amazon-api-gateway-private-endpoints/) feature requires creating a [resource policy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/apigateway-private-apis.html#apigateway-private-api-set-up-resource-policy) that allows API requests coming from a VPC.

AFAICT there is no way to configure the `Policy` field on [`AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi`](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-apigateway-restapi.html#cfn-apigateway-restapi-policy) via SAM. (Additionally, I observe my manually-added resource policies getting wiped on new SAM deployments.)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create an API via SAM, setting `EndpointConfiguration: PRIVATE`

**Observed result:**
No resource policy on the API.

**Expected result:**
No resource policy on the API until SAM allows configuring a resource policy for an API in the SAM template.

issue_title: Fix navigation drawbacks in documentation, create a logo
issue_body:
The documentation has a substantial drawback when it comes to navigating through content.

### Problem example

See the [Contributing](https://pythonhosted.org/behave-django/contribute.html) chapter. How do you get back to the welcome page, for example?

This issue seems to be inherent to the Alabaster theme. Other projects have the same problem, see e.g. the [behave docs](http://pythonhosted.org/behave/django.html), but it's less relevant there because the docs have the behave "logo" (the image with the words box) that takes you home. We should probably have something similar.

- Can we fix the scarce navigation via some configuration switch? ([Sphinx](http://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/stable/config.html#confval-html_sidebars), [Alabaster](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/alabaster))
- We should probably come up with a (Pony) logo and add it as a "home link" to the docs.

### Logo ideas

* [Yet unused Django mascots](http://mirskytech.com/django-iconography-pony-critter/) (Andrew Mirsky)
* Combination of Django and behave. (A Pony and Given-When-Then)

_"Given a pony, when I run behave with behave-django, then my project has awesome tests."_

issue_title: set up CI
Can this be trained for political groups/committees?
issue_body:
It may be that this is not quite the right tool for us, but I wanted to check.

I have a list of ~60,000,000 names from a campaign financing and lobbying activity database from the state of California. There are many, many duplicates, but there are also many malformed rows. The names are supposed to be divided up into "name-last", 'name-first", "name-suffix" and "name-title" but they are most often not. I think we want do a few things with pp:

1) put the names together (most people just use the names this way and ignore any weirdness), and then use pp to separate them and see if they separate out the same, or fix the data with the separated forms.

2) separate out fields when the "name-last" is used for the entire name, as is extremely common.

3) identify names when they are part of an organization name.

An example of what I am seeing now is this:
```
>>> import probablepeople
>>> 
>>> probablepeople.parse('Aaron Starr for Oxnard City Council')
[('Aaron', 'CorporationName'), ('Starr', 'CorporationName'), ('for', 'CorporationName'), ('Oxnard', 'CorporationName'), ('City', 'CorporationName'), ('Council', 'CorporationNameOrganization')]
>>> 
>>> probablepeople.parse('Aaron Read & Associates')
[('Aaron', 'CorporationName'), ('Read', 'CorporationName'), ('&', 'CorporationName'), ('Associates', 'CorporationNameOrganization')]
>>> 
>>> probablepeople.parse('Aaron Klein')
[('Aaron', 'GivenName'), ('Klein', 'Surname')]
>>>
```
It would have been really nice to see the following, if I could have my wish. Is there any way to get this behavior or something similar? And is there a reason "for" and "&" are 'CorporationName' and not something like a connector or conjunction? Any suggestions would be appreciated.
```
>>> import probablepeople
>>> 
>>> probablepeople.parse('Aaron Starr for Oxnard City Council')
[('Aaron', 'GivenName'), ('Starr', 'Surname'), ('for', 'Connector'), ('Oxnard', 'CorporationName'), ('City', 'CorporationName'), ('Council', 'CorporationNameOrganization')]
>>> 
>>> probablepeople.parse('Aaron Read & Associates')
[('Aaron', 'GivenName'), ('Read', 'Surname'), ('&', 'Connector'), ('Associates', 'CorporationNameOrganization')]
>>> 
```
FYI, I am also looking at cjdd3b/fec-standardizer. Perhaps that might be easier to use. We will see.

Develop branch + vuex 3 upgrades

issue_title: ApplicationError fails when mixing python2 and python3
issue_body:
I've encountered a crash when using a Python2 router with a Python3 client.

Indeed, when the router send a `autobahn.wamp.message.Error` containing kwargs the client crashes:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/autobahn_sync/.tox/py34/lib/python3.4/site-packages/autobahn/wamp/websocket.py", line 90, in onMessage
    self._session.onMessage(msg)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/autobahn_sync/.tox/py34/lib/python3.4/site-packages/autobahn_sync/session.py", line 32, in onMessage
    return super(_AsyncSession, self).onMessage(msg)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/autobahn_sync/.tox/py34/lib/python3.4/site-packages/autobahn/wamp/protocol.py", line 921, in onMessage
    txaio.reject(on_reply, self._exception_from_message(msg))
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/autobahn_sync/.tox/py34/lib/python3.4/site-packages/autobahn/wamp/protocol.py", line 248, in _exception_from_message
    if msg.kwargs:
TypeError: __init__() keywords must be strings
```

The trouble is this kwargs's keys are made of bytes (i.e. python2's `str`) but python3 wants unicode (python3's `str`) to use them as function parameters
```
(Pdb) msg.kwargs
{b'message': 'event history for the given subscription is not available or enabled'}
```

The WAMP spec (https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-oberstet-hybi-tavendo-wamp-02#section-5.2) only define the utf8-encoded string, I guess then everything should be in UTF-8.
Given there is no trouble when using Python3 everywhere,  that mean the data's encoding depends of the sender which I believe is a bug both at serialization (should only send data in one encoding) and deserialization (should only accept data in one encoding) time.

issue_title: Dependency on `six` can result in broken underlying SDK install, `pip` incorrectly deduces that requirements are satisfied
issue_body:
Technically we've done nothing wrong here, but we still might want to fix this.

Try the following steps:
```shell
$ virtualenv venv
$ soure venv/bin/activate
$ pip install six==1.0.0
$ pip install globus-cli
```

The CLI install finishes, but the results are bad. Because `globus-cli` depends on `six>=1.0.0,<2.0.0`, `pip` deduces that there's no need to upgrade that dependency.
However, `globus-sdk` depends on `six>=1.10.0,<2.0.0` because of some additions to `six.moves`, so the CLI crashes with an `ImportError` during startup.

`pip` should identify the requirement and either upgrade (as it would if we were installing `globus-sdk` into the virtualenv directly), or fail.

This whole state of affairs is irksome. It seems that we should maybe specify `six>=1.10.0,<2.0.0` in the CLI requirements(?).

issue_title: Check if we can initialize the PeerPool earlier
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

This is a follow up to the discussion @pipermerriam and I had [here](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/pull/867#discussion_r193187256).

Currently the `PeerPool` isn't available until some other async tasks are resolved. This issue serves as a reminder to check whether the `PeerPool` could be created synchronously earlier.

### How can it be fixed

A possible idea would be to create the `PeerPool` early on without the dependencies that first need to be resolved asynchronously and then as a second step, once these dependencies are resolved, call some initialization API on the `PeerPool`. Before that, the `PeerPool` would already exist but would reside in some sort of `bootstrapping` mode until that API is called.

Not sure, if I like that tbh. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

issue_title: Variable-Property Mapping (VPM)
issue_body:
A VPM helps interlingual applications to harmonize the variable properties from one grammar to the next. E.g., Grammar A might put TENSE information at E.TENSE with a finite range of values, while Grammar B combines it with ASPECT and MOOD at E.TAM, and Grammar C doesn't use TENSE at all. The VPM will help normalize these values to a shared feature geometry.

See: http://moin.delph-in.net/RmrsVpm

pyDelphin should be able to load a .vpm file and apply it to *MRSs forwards and backwards (note that roundtripping will not necessarily be lossless).

issue_title: 4.2 final check list
issue_body:
- [x] Put binstar token handling back in.
- [x] `!top` and `!bottom` for lists
- [x] Consider `!final`, `!enforce`, `!override` instead of `!important`.
- [x] Messaging on what parameters are coming from where.
- [x] More robust validation error messaging.


Shoot for these too:

- [x] Further integrate `context` into cli execute logic (get rid of the need for using `args` so much).
- [x] Get test coverage back up.
- [x] Better docstring documentation of fresh code.
- [x] Make environment variables splittable into lists (with a separator).

issue_title: Target vehicle is fixed
issue_body: The API defaults to controlling vehicle 0, this needs to be changeable by the user.

issue_title: VariantHeader__str__ returns non-ascii characters
issue_body:
Calling str(VariantFile.header) returns something that looks like binary noise. Looking at the code, I think this is a simple bug:

    def __str__(self):
        cdef int hlen
        cdef char *hstr = bcf_hdr_fmt_text(self.ptr, 0, &hlen)

        ret = hstr[:hlen]
        free(hstr)
        return force_str(hstr)

So *hstr* is freed and *ret* is ignored.

issue_title: Allow for attaching/detaching multiple disks with a single call
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When a volume uses multiple disks then we'd want to detach and attach the entire set in the same call instead of running requests for each disk in a loop. A single detach/attach can take 3-4 minutes so larger volumes with several disks, say 6-8 disk per volume, result in a 40-60 minute wait time for sequential detach or attach operations.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Azure CLI should support a function that takes a list of disks as a parameter for detach and attach operations.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The Azure CLI calls attach_managed_data_disk which in turn runs get_vm, vm.storage_profile.data_disks.append then set_vm. The alternative would be to loop for an array parameter and append each element before calling set_vm.

**Additional context**
Below az_disk_attach_vol is a wrapper for 'az vm disk attach' and CPYARCH uses 8 disks.
If az_disk_attach_vol uses sequential calls to AZ CLI then we wait #disk * operation time.

issue_title: List compute Plans
issue_body:
For certain images, we are receiving an error when provisioning Virtual Machines:

    Azure Error: VMMarketplaceInvalidInput Message: Creating a virtual machine from Marketplace image requires Plan information in the request.

VirtualMachines have a [parameter for Plan](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-mgmt-compute/azure/mgmt/compute/compute/v2016_04_30_preview/models/virtual_machine.py#L31). And I see [a model for Plan](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-mgmt-compute/azure/mgmt/compute/compute/v2016_04_30_preview/models/plan.py), but no operations.

How can we list available plans so we know what information to pass to the VirtualMachine's create_or_update()? 
How can we know which images require a plan? It looks like the VirtualMachineImage model has a plan parameter, but does that mean there is only one valid plan for that image?
For images that require a plan, how can we see which plans are valid for that image?

issue_title: Logging send/receive data at DEBUG level
issue_body:
This issue is result of aio-libs google group [discussion](https://groups.google.com/d/topic/aio-libs/4WOi3b_XNHY/discussion). Since often developer have no server-side code/protocol/api, but trying to reverse client-side by comparing requests/responses, ability to «see what happend» is very important. For insecure connections there is wireshark, but most of services are secure nowadays.
Easiest way is to wrap transport, but I don't really know how this will affect performance.

issue_title: Move exome tool functions to tool library
GeoMesa kit
issue_body:
Dear all,

as mentioned on the mailing list, I have been working on a GeoMesa kit to provide basic GIS functionality.

It currently uses shapely to create arbitrary shapes for agents and patches (agents that do not move). Shapes can also be imported from GeoJSON. 
Adapting any shape-to-shape interaction implemented in shapely will be straight-forward. 

I have also written a map visualization that uses leaflet and draws the shapes on top of it. You can click on any shape to get a pop-up of the agents/patches attribute (which are potentially imported from the GeoJSON)

There is still quite a lot to do in terms of feature-completeness, documentation and testing.

However, I won't be able to work on this until September. If someone wants to contribute I have created [a branch on my fork of mesa](https://github.com/Corvince/mesa/tree/GeoMesa). I don't know if it makes sense at this stage already to pull this into the main repository (as a branch!), but GitHub does not allow to create a pull request for a new branch. 

Have fun with it
Corvin

issue_title: Stop relying on CDN for TinyMCE
issue_body:
The CDN version of TinyMCE has been broken for the last week or so - see https://github.com/tinymce/tinymce/issues/4171 . This has broken the dashboard for any site that uses the default dashboard setup - and with our current setup there is no way to fix this other than to monkey-patch `dashboard/layout.html` and use a different CDN.

I think this highlights the risks of using a CDN where we cannot control the minor version of the package (there is no way to specify minor version for that CDN). The maintainers of TinyMCE offer a different CDN (cloud.tinymce.com) as an alternative, but this one requires you to sign up for an API key.

I think it would be better for Oscar to provide a local copy of TinyMCE to avoid such issues in future.

issue_title: Review all cryptographic algorithm and parameter defaults
issue_body:
There are many places where the Let's Encrypt client will generate cryptographic keys or parameters, or set or use cryptographic parameter or algorithm defaults (including defining key sizes and ciphersuite defaults for servers in which we enable TLS!).

We need a thorough review of whether all of our choices are good, not least because they may become the TLS configuration defaults for millions of Internet servers.

I started a page to collect references to what other people have had to say about ciphersuites at

https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/wiki/Ciphersuite-guidance

That mostly doesn't include information about choices for other kinds of parameter and keylength options, but in a few places it does (and maybe we can put more of that kind of material in there).

Cc @jhalderm.

persistently breaks the client, even when subsequently run with an ordinary

`sudo ~/venv/bin/letsencrypt run`

issue_title: Question: Is it possible to configure a consumer to not automatically reconnect?
issue_body:
This may seem like an odd question, but I have a need to better control when a consumer reconnects. I have an application with many consumers, and I need to ensure that they all connect and (more importantly) reconnect in a very orderly manner.  I am aware of `reconnect.backoff.jitter.ms` but this doesn't help as my consumers are in separate processes, and I need to coordinate their reconnections over time.

As I see it, there are two possibilities that would help:
- A way to configure the consumer to never reconnect automatically. I would also need to be aware of when the consumer became disconnected (throwing an error from `poll()` would be fine).
- Failing that, a way to become aware that a consumer has been disconnected, and is about to reconnect. This would allow me to manually intervene to shut down the consumer and restart it at a time that is appropriate.

After digging around the docs a little, I don't see that either of these things are possible, but I'm hoping you can prove me wrong 😉

issue_title: Searching for node/nodejs in sandboxjs.py broken
issue_body:
## Expected Behavior
Checking for the existence of nodejs or node should not throw an exception.

## Actual Behavior
The code that checks for the presence of node/nodejs does not catch the correct exception when a given binary is not present. It should just gracefully catch the exception an move on with its life.

## Proposed fix
Change line 81 of sandboxjs.py from:
```python
        except OSError as e:
            if e.errno == errno.ENOENT:
                pass
            else:
                raise
```
to:
```python
        except OSError as e:
            if e.errno == errno.ENOENT or e.errno == errno.EACCES:                                                                                                                                                          
                pass
            else:
                raise
```

issue_title: Circle marker creation is slow
issue_body:
#### The code sample

```python
import folium

map_ = folium.Map()

for i in range(1000):
    folium.features.Circle([i, i],
                           radius=3,
                           color='blue',
                           fill_color='blue',
                           fill_opacity=1,
                           fill=True).add_to(map_)
```
#### Problem description

Circle creation proceeds roughly at the order of 100 per second, which is quite slow. Creating a map with thousands of circle markers (which is handled easily by the browser) becomes slightly annoying. 

#### Expected Output

I would expect above code to run 10-1000 times faster.

#### Output of ``folium.__version__``
0.5.0

#### Extras
I tried to quickly check what is the bottleneck, and it's the jinja template compilation, which is executed 4133 times.

![circle](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1212244/34559260-7993ee10-f141-11e7-90ad-111e0fe01bca.png)

issue_title: Circle radius units
issue_body:
The folium documentation for a circle says that the radius parameter is "Circle radius in pixels"

http://folium.readthedocs.org/en/latest/#circle-markers

The live example shows that it's calling L.circle() JavaScript method and passing the radius to that method.

Jumping over to Leaflet documentation, it says that radius is "... radius of a circle. Units are in meters."

http://leafletjs.com/reference.html#circle

So isn't the radius passed to folium actually the radius in meters? If that's the case, I can submit a PR to update the documentation. Otherwise, am I missing a conversion somewhere?

issue_body:
Currently the bash prompt showing as colour version, but with no glyphs, i.e.:

    ?username???/???opt???path1???path2???

`vim` is working as expected; the correct glyph for the hard separators.  

**The expected glyphs *are* visible if I `cat` the `powerline.json` files with the hard separator codes.**

Originally it wasn't displaying within vim, until I installed a patched font for for putty.  Even then it still wasn't visible on the shell prompt, however I at least go the *question marks to show* by setting the locale (see towards the end) and re-copying the main config:

This is inside a docker instance using a centos7 image, with powerline installed using `pip` as per the setup instructions of the main powerline git repository. I did have to add the locale to the bare bones image:

    localedef -i en_GB -c -f UTF-8 en_GB.UTF-8

    LANG=en_GB.utf-8

I appreciate any help to try and fix this, and any tips on how to help diagnose the issue (even code edits) is appreciated.

issue_title: Searches 'GET' with a body fails when elastic search is behind squid
issue_body:
We have elasticseach behind squid and the debug 'curl' command fails:
e.g.
curl -XGET 'http://elk.example.com:80/_search?size=10' -d '{"sort": {"@timestamp": {"order": "desc"}}, "query": {"query_string": {"query": "message:\"Build timed out\" AND message:\"Marking the build as failed.\" AND tags:\"console\" AND build_name:\"gate-openstack-project-dsvm-commit\""}}}'

<title>ERROR: The requested URL could not be retrieved</title>

However, the same command without the -d body (which is ignored for HTTP GET requests) works just fine and returns the expected result. 

I have a simple patch fix I'll submit, but I agree it might be a squid bug/misconfiguration.

issue_title: support table as target in criterions
issue_body: In reinforcement learning, we sometimes need a (action, reward) pair as target. Currently we have to squeeze the pair into one Tensor and feed to criterion as target, then in criterion we use select to split the two. But it is not efficient as the tensor won't be contiguous. Need a table so that 2 contiguous vectors can be passed into criterion.

issue_title: Overwrite hyper params when resume training
issue_body: In resume training, user needs to provide model and optim state (--model and --state ) of a snapshot for BigDL to resume the training from. It's often the case that user needs to adjust hyper-parameters in optim state (e.g. learning rate schedule, weight decay) in the resumed training. But in current implementations (as in googlenet example), overwriting hyper params in state file is not convenient. Should allow command line configurations to overwrite the configs in state.

issue_title: Add basic options for ResourceRelatedField
issue_body:
According to the JSONAPI specification, if I have an `Article` model with a to-many relationship to a `Comment` model and I want to express this when I retrieve an `Article` resource, there are multiple valid ways I can do this:

1. Include the comments by default in the response, having a list of resource identifier objects in the `relationships` object and a list with the actual resource objects in the `included` object.
2. Return a list of resource identifier objects in the `relationships` object only, without including the actual attributes of the comments.
3. Include only a link (e.g. `/articles/1/comments`) in the `relationships` object.

I have a model that links to a lot of related objects so the 3rd option would be the best for me, but I can't configure the ResourceRelatedField to act like that. It always returns a `data` object with the full list of related resource identifiers. Is there a way I can avoid this? Or do I have to extend the class myself? Doesn't seem very trivial to do that in a clean way.

Ideally, since the JSONAPI specs accept these 3 options, I think this library should allow these 3 configurations out of the box.

issue_title: [Feature Request] Integration with Git
issue_body:
Hi and thanks for the awesome framework, really happy to see you picked up the development of it again!

One thing I was thinking about that would be useful feature to introduce is Git integration. Basically, when running and experiment execute the following commands:

- `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` - get the branch you are in
- `git rev-parse HEAD` - get the commit you are at
- `git status`
- `git diff`

This way we could *store additional info about experiments* - sometimes not ALL experiment parameters are passed through arguments (e.g. part of python code you changed just to test out a different setting, without actually pulling that to the experiment config).

Another huge benefit of this would be *fully reproducible experiment code* - you can always checkout a specific commit, apply the diff and re-run the experiment in the same setup, even if you changed the source code in the meantime.

What do you think? Happy for feedback and also willing to contribute to this one if you think it makes sense.

Best,
Marko

issue_title: Better tokenizer for Chinese

Currently, we support using the MosesTokenizer in NLTK and the spaCy tokenizer. Both are not very good for tokenizing Chinese sentences. We could consider adding some Chinese tokenizers.

              (ite (= n1_p P0) 1 0)
              (ite (= n2_p P0) 1 0)
              (ite (= n3_p P0) 1 0)))

              (ite (= n0_p P1) 1 0)
              (ite (= n1_p P1) 1 0)
              (ite (= n2_p P1) 1 0)

issue_title: On Windows, `open_fs('C:')` opens the wrong directory
issue_body:
On Windows, `open_fs('C:')` is expected to open the root directory of the drive mapped to 'C'. Instead, it opens the working directory. This does not happen for other disks.

```
>>> fs.open_fs('C:')
OSFS('C:\\Python36')
>>> fs.open_fs('C:/')
OSFS('C:\\')
>>> fs.open_fs('D:')
OSFS('D:\\')
>>> fs.open_fs('D:/')
OSFS('D:\\')
```

issue_title: DRONEKIT_PATH variable
issue_body:
Currently dronekit only supports relative and absolute paths. It would be nice if we could point `api start <file>` to look at a set of directories when running a script. DRONEKIT_PATH would point to your project folders.

This idea was brought up in a recent dronekit session Chris and I had. At a minimum we should specify in the documentation that if that user can't specify the directory in which mavproxy is started(i.e. running SITL), then they must start use an absolute path to call their script.

DRONEKIT_PATH should not be necessary for when we run dronekit without mavproxy. But at a minimum we should document that the user should provide an absolute path when running dronekit scripts.

A DOS featurizer that is a 16-element vector of the various s, p, d, f overlaps

i.e., s-s interaction is the square of the s-projected DOS, s-p interaction is the multiplication of the s projected and p-projected DOS, etc

issue_title: analysis: Thread1.1 5.1.7 Minimum Supported Children – IPv6 Datagram Buffering
issue_body:
According to Conformance Document requirement for Router device:

- It should support minimal 10 children (at least 6 SEDs)

- Thread Router must be able to buffer at least one 1280-octet IPv6 datagram destined for an attached SED

- Thread Router must be able to buffer at least one 106-octet IPv6 datagram per attached SED

Test case 5.1.7 assumes that there are 12 thread devices in network (1 Leader + 1 DUT as Router + 10 children: 4ED and 6 SED). Currently we support maximum 5 children by default and allocate 64 message buffers totally, 128bytes size per buffer. For supporting 10 children, maybe we need more memory allocation for messages of buffering above IPv6 datagram.
