issue_title: Round-trip GeoJSON
issue_body:
I would like to be able to round-trip GeoJSON through csvkit, so that small operations can be done using csvkit in the middle, without resulting in strange diffs.  https://github.com/wireservice/csvkit/pull/867 is part of this effort.

i.e.  The following should have very minimal output.
```
$ wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lyzidiamond/learn-geojson/master/geojson/pdxplaces.geojson
$ in2csv -f geojson pdxplaces.geojson > pdxplaces.csv
$ csvjson --lon longitude --lat latitude --indent=2 pdxplaces.csv > pdxplaces.csv.geojson
$ diff -u pdxplaces.geojson pdxplaces.csv.geojson
```

Some of the diff results which need to be controllable using command line args:

1. sequence of keys in a feature ; i.e. should `geometry` appear before `properties` or the opposite.  It seems different tools make different choices for this ordering, and ideally `in2csv` can annotate its output with this ordering (assuming it is consistent throughout the input) so that csvjson can re-use the same ordering.
2. do not emit empty properties.  Currently they are being emitted and mostly they shouldn't be emitted.
3. generation of the bbox.  It should be possible to disable this being computed and added, and it is non-trivial to compute it for complex geojson (c.f. https://github.com/wireservice/csvkit/pull/867).

After simplic solutions for those three, the diff looks like:

```diff

issue_title: Difference in behavior with inheritance across modules between Windows and MacOS/Linux
issue_body:
I've encountered another issue while porting Audio ToolKit to pybind11.
There are several modules defined in the library, all driving from a parent class in a module named Core.
On Windows, I defined in the Core bindings the Python declaration fo the base class as such: https://github.com/mbrucher/AudioTK/blob/feature/pybind11/Python/Core/Core.cpp
After, in the EQ module, I declared the inheritance: https://github.com/mbrucher/AudioTK/blob/feature/pybind11/Python/EQ/EQ.cpp and when running tests, Python/pybind11 could find all the links and everything was fine.
On macOS, I got this: ImportError: generic_type: type "FloatSecondOrderBaseCoefficients" referenced unknown base type "ATK::TypedBaseFilter<float, float>"
"ATK::TypedBaseFilter<float, float>" is a type defined in the Core, and strangely, pybind11 couldn't find the definition in its memory, even if I force the import of Core before.

How is inheritance across modules supposed to work? Am I doing something crazy that no one did before?

issue_title: Create exception for the ssl certification
certificate symlinks not fixed up on upgrade.
issue_body:
I installed and used letsencrypt via git. The last commit was:

commit 4d4cfb414f2847f1814cfe2949c6e76f112e4ade
Author: Brad Warren <bradmwarren@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 5 17:55:18 2016 -0700

    Release 0.5.0

This on Debian Jessie:

root@chaffee:~/letsencrypt# lsb_release -a
No LSB modules are available.
Distributor ID:	Debian
Description:	Debian GNU/Linux 8.5 (jessie)
Release:	8.5
Codename:	jessie
root@chaffee:~/letsencrypt#

I was able to obtain a certificate and all went well.

At the end of July, I installed certbot from the debian repo:

root@chaffee:~/letsencrypt# pre certbot
certbot	0.8.1-2~bpo8+1		all
python-certbot	0.8.1-2~bpo8+1		all
python-certbot-apache	0.8.1-1~bpo8+1		all

(pre is a tool for seeing what packages I have installed.)

The certificate updated, but Apache did not serve up the new certificate. I narrowed the problem down to the symlinks in /etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info/: they were not updated.

root@chaffee:/etc/apache2# ll /etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info/*
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 37 May  3 09:30 /etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info/cert.pem -> ../../archive/wli.ddns.info/cert2.pem
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 38 May  3 09:30 /etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info/chain.pem -> ../../archive/wli.ddns.info/chain2.pem
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 42 May  3 09:30 /etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info/fullchain.pem -> ../../archive/wli.ddns.info/fullchain2.pem
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 40 May  3 09:30 /etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info/privkey.pem -> ../../archive/wli.ddns.info/privkey2.pem
root@chaffee:/etc/apache2# ll /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/*
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1793 Feb  6 10:22 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/cert1.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1793 May  3 09:30 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/cert2.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1793 Jul 27 09:51 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/cert3.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1675 Feb  6 10:22 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/chain1.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1647 May  3 09:30 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/chain2.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1647 Jul 27 09:51 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/chain3.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 3468 Feb  6 10:22 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/fullchain1.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 3440 May  3 09:30 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/fullchain2.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 3440 Jul 27 09:51 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/fullchain3.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1704 Feb  6 10:22 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/privkey1.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1704 May  3 09:30 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/privkey2.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1708 Jul 27 09:51 /etc/letsencrypt/archive/wli.ddns.info/privkey3.pem
root@chaffee:/etc/apache2#

The following series of commands repaired the problem, and the server is now serving up the new certificate, as expected.

root@chaffee:/etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info# rm cert.pem 
root@chaffee:/etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info# ln -s ../../archive/wli.ddns.info/cert3.pem  cert.pem
root@chaffee:/etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info# rm chain.pem 
root@chaffee:/etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info# ln -s ../../archive/wli.ddns.info/chain3.pem chain.pem
root@chaffee:/etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info# rm fullchain.pem 
root@chaffee:/etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info# ln -s ../../archive/wli.ddns.info/fullchain3.pem fullchain.pem
root@chaffee:/etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info# rm privkey.pem 
root@chaffee:/etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info# ln -s ../../archive/wli.ddns.info/privkey3.pem privkey.pem
root@chaffee:/etc/letsencrypt/live/wli.ddns.info#

I would hope that git repo users would upgrade to the packages. This extra step may help.

issue_title: Expose API usage
issue_body:
The Salesforce API returns a usage report in the header of each response, as detailed in the documentation: https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api_rest.meta/api_rest/headers_api_usage.htm
it looks something like `Sforce-Limit-Info: api-usage=18/5000`. 

It would be very helpful to be able to track our API usage, but it doesn't look like the simple-salesforce library exposes this information. I'd like to add a variable to instances of `Salesforce` objects that gets updated in `_call_salesforce()`, and potentially a similar variable in `SFType` objects that transfers to the `Salesforce` object in the `__getattr__` method. Are there any other methods I should account for in this change?

issue_title: bug 1407380 - Add error message on python crash
issue_body:
At [this line](https://github.com/mozilla-releng/scriptworker/blob/46f913f/scriptworker/task.py#L175-L177), we print `"exit code: {}".format(exitcode)` to the logfile.

In [bug 1398800](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1398800), we're hitting a lot of `exit code: -11`. This is an issue we need to address elsewhere, but in the meantime, we can add an informative error message that will be more easily spotted.

Let's do something like

```
if exitcode == -11:
    status_line = "Automation Error: python exited with signal {}".format(exitcode)
```

before the `log.info` and `print` at the bottom of that block.

issue_title: Feature request snapshot filter
issue_body:
If you encounter a bug use the following template.
If you have a feature request feel free to freestyle.

### System
**qcodes branch**
qcodes/dataset

**feature**
The snapshot object contains all the parameters present in all devices in the setup. I guess for most setups that would mean hundreds of different parameters. Of these, we generally have special interest into some. I propose to add an extra property to the DataContainer, i.e. MetaFilters. This property has the following form:
{'filtername_abc':
['path_to_a_meta_data_end_point', ['path_to_b_meta_data_end_point', ['path_to_c_meta_data_end_point'],
'filtername_def':
['path_to_d_meta_data_end_point', ['path_to_e_meta_data_end_point', ['path_to_f_meta_data_end_point']}
The metadata filter can be used to identify the important parameters active in a setup. For example, we have voltage source with 50 channels, but for the presently loaded sample, we only use a subset of channels. In the filter we can identify the used channels so, later on, we can easily ask for only those metadata endpoints.

- do an inband shutdown of a partition (e.g. via linux shell:  shutdown -P now)
- partition state switches "Paused"
- try to start that partition using "Daily - Start"

This results in the following error on the UI
"STS00005E
One or more targeted partitions are already started. Only partitions in the Stopped state can be started.
To restart partitions, first run the Stop task, and then try the operation again."


The cli does not care about this issue at all, it just leaves the partition state to paused:
$ zhmc partition list P000S67B
/+-------------------------------------------------------------+------------+
| name                                                        | status     |
|-------------------------------------------------------------+------------|
| andreas_cloud_init_tests                                    | paused     |

$ zhmc partition start P000S67B andreas_cloud_init_tests

$ zhmc partition list P000S67B
/+-------------------------------------------------------------+------------+
| name                                                        | status     |
|-------------------------------------------------------------+------------|
| andreas_cloud_init_tests                                    | paused     |


The python binding seems to behave like the cli. It returns immediately even if wait_for_completion ==True. There's no indication that the operation failed.

### Expected behavior

issue_title: Add support for showroom-live.com
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [ ] This is a bug report.
- [x] This is a plugin request.
- [ ] This is a feature request.
- [x] I used the search function to find already opened/closed issues or pull requests.

### Description

Showroom is a Japanese streaming site, used by artists, entertainers, and amateur streamers. It does not provide VOD services.

### Expected behaviour:

For this code:

    >>> import streamlink
    >>> streams = streamlink.streams("https://www.showroom-live.com/48_Tomu_Mutou")

To return a list of streams (or None if she's not streaming)

### Actual behaviour

Raises `streamlink.exceptions.NoPluginError`

### [Working Plugin](https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/compare/master...wlerin:master?diff=unified&name=master)

I have written a Showroom plugin, and tested that it correctly returns the desired streams. Some additional testing is required before I create a pull request, but I thought I'd open an issue first and maybe get general feedback or even help resolving some of the issues below.

### API Urls
More API urls besides those used in the proposed plugin:

- [Sample Event Room List](https://www.showroom-live.com/event/all_rooms?event_id=1364)
- [Live broadcasts w/ streaming links](https://www.showroom-live.com/api/live/onlives)
- [Upcoming lives in the Idol genre](https://www.showroom-live.com/api/live/upcoming?genre_id=102)
- [Next live for a room](https://www.showroom-live.com/api/room/next_live?room_id=61576)
- [Video Caption](https://www.showroom-live.com/api/live/telop?room_id=61627)
- [Comment Log](https://www.showroom-live.com/api/live/comment_log?room_id=61627)
- [Ranking Summary](https://www.showroom-live.com/api/live/summary_ranking?room_id=61627)


### Unresolved Issues

#### 1. Caption
Live rooms usually have a "telop" or caption, that displays at the top of the video and usually describes whatever's going on in the room that day. The plugin stores this, but does nothing with it. Possibly it could be muxed with the stream like a subtitle? More investigation required.

#### 2. Stream Title
In relation to Hubcapp's title-related work, there are five options for determing a room's title:
1. The "room_url_key", taken either from _get_stream_info, or the first url pattern. This key is meaningful for official rooms, but random and meaningless for amateur rooms.
2. The actual page title. This is almost always in Japanese.
3. The "telop" or caption, displayed at the top of a live video. Also almost always in Japanese.
4. A translation of the title or telop. Providing this in streamlink itself is unfeasible.
5. The numerical room_id. This is as meaningless to humans as the amateur room titles.

Currently, the plugin uses 1., although in one specific case (when the second url pattern is used and the stream title is requested before _get_streams is called) this requires fetching the same data from the api twice.

#### 3. VR Streams
Showroom provides no support for VR on PC. There are VR streams viewable on their mobile app, but even if it could capture the necessary streaming urls, the raw video needs to be remapped to a different projection.

#### 4. HLS Support
The one time I attempted to record the HLS sources with FFmpeg, the result was a catastrophe (vast majority of every video recorded was missing). The problem was probably on my end but I did not investigate further. I have not tested them at all using this plugin.

#### 5. Unstable RTMP Streams
Using FFmpeg or MPV to record/view the rtmp streams results in frequent disconnections for some less stable streams. It would be useful to keep restarting these until the API reports the live has finished, but this is probably not a job for the Showroom plugin. It already won't return streams once the live ends, but in my experiments with FFmpeg a recording started exactly when the stream ends sometimes hangs indefinitely. Occasionally during a particularly bad stream it also enters a "ping loop of death", where it just keeps pinging the server and never receives any data. I handle this manually in another script I wrote specifically for Showroom, but I don't think this plugin can do anything about it.

#### 6. The 720p Mystery
On very rare occasions, a Showroom stream will be available in 720p. I never had the foresight to check the api data when that happens, and it's impossible to tell from the web player when this is the case. To account for this...

#### 7. Stream Weighting
I know of no way to accurately determine the height of an rtmp stream. All the API provides is "original spec" and "low spec"--usually 360p and 198p. However, because of the mystery 720p streams, which are almost certainly "original spec", I have set the weighting for "original spec" to 720, and that for "low spec" to 198. This may cause difficulties with the accurately-reported HLS frame heights (or not-so-accurately: low for HLS reports as 160p)

#### 8. Events, Campaigns, Schedule, Onlive
[Event pages](https://www.showroom-live.com/event/daruma_big), [Campaign pages](https://www.showroom-live.com/campaign/akb48_sr), and the [Schedule](https://www.showroom-live.com/timetable) and [Onlive](https://www.showroom-live.com/onlive) pages all provide lists of rooms, almost like a playlist. It would be nice if it were possible to parse these, but it may be beyond the scope of streamlink.

#### 9. Comments
Probably beyond the scope of streamlink, but I've always wanted to record the comments alongside videos.

issue_title: How do I duck-type a class to behave like a DOMWidget?
issue_body:
**TLDR**
What is the recommended way to duck-type a class to behave like a DOMWidget?

**Details**
I have a class (`Foo`) that should be a custom `DOMWidget`; but, because of numerous miscellaneous problems from multiple inheritance, cannot inherit from `ipywidgets.DOMWidget`.

Rather, I have another class (`WidgetBar`) that inherits from `DOMWidget` and am trying to delegate, from `Foo` to `WidgetBar` whenever DOMWidget-like behavior is required out of `Foo`.

I am trying to get this done through duck-typing and have identified the following methods to be defined in the class - 

- `_widget_to_json`
- `_handle_displayed`

```
class Foo(parent):
   ....
   def __init__(self):
           self.widget_bar = WidgetBar()

   def _widget_to_json(self):
          return self.widget_bar._widget_to_json()
   ....
```

Rather than handling all required methods like this on Foo directyly, is there an attribute/a method that a class should have for it to be accepted as a `DOMWidget` and through which all delegations can be handled in a single-go? So, Foo can have this method defined to be - 

```
def _as_dom_widget(self):
    return self.widget_bar
```
If not, is there a list of all methods that have to be defined in `Foo` for its instance to work like a `DOMWidget` under all circumstances?

issue_title: Any way to calculate or estimate number of blocks before/while writing them?
issue_body:
Thanks very much for all your work on the library!

I'm running dedupe on a large (2.6M) set of records and it's taking a while. Is there any way to see in advance how many blocks will be generated in the block writing step? (or get an estimate?) In some of my initial runs it was going up to 350k, the last one it got up to 1.5M.

Also do you have any suggestions for speeding it up? I'm basically following the MySQL example (but using Postgres) and my fields look like the following:

    {'field' : 'fullname', 'type': 'Name'},
    {'field' : 'initials', 'type': 'Custom', 'comparator': mutual_subsumes},
    # initials is a field extracted from the 'fullname' field, mutual_subsumes is a custom function to
    # check whether both sets of initials contain initials that are not in the other set (e.g. 'a,b,c' and 
    # 'a,b,d' fails but 'a,b,c' and 'a,b' passes)
    {'field' : 'givenname', 'type': 'String', 'has missing' : True}, # the rest of the fields are frequently null

issue_title: Turn logger and config into singleton classes
issue_body:
It seems that logger and config are used very often in many different python files and classes. It would nice to avoid having passing around logger and config everywhere as arguements. We should make them into singleton classes where we do something like this in any python script:

```
from config import Config
x = Config()
num_workers = x.num_workers
```

issue_title: RFC statuses and oauthlib roadmaps
issue_body:
Currently, oauthlib feature list is maintained here: https://oauthlib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/feature_matrix.html

We can improve it by showing all "OAuth"-related RFCs, and the status/roadmap for each (if not applicable, or "won't do", say it). Also, we can split RFCs bullet points when too big.

I'm starting the list of them for OAuth2:
- rfc6749 [The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749)
- rfc6750 [The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework: Bearer Token Usage](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750)
- rfc7009 [OAuth 2.0 Token Revocation](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7009)
- rfc7662 [OAuth 2.0 Token Introspection](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7662)
- rfc7636 [Proof Key for Code Exchange by OAuth Public Clients](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7636)
- rfc6819 [OAuth 2.0 Threat Model and Security Considerations](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6819)
- rfc7519 [JSON Web Token (JWT)](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519)
- rfc7523 [JSON Web Token (JWT) Profile for OAuth 2.0 Client Authentication and Authorization Grants](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7523)
- rfc8252 [OAuth 2.0 for Native Apps](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8252)
- openid 1.0 e1 [OpenID Connect Core 1.0 errset1](http://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html)

Statuses proposed:
- `done` or `since x.y.z`
- `in progress`
- `won't do` / `n/a`
- `contributor friendly`

In either cases, we can link to an github issue or discussions if any `#123`.
The project become bigger and having visibility on the current states would be great. I understand it takes some time to maintain this list, but that's for the benefit of the newcomers also.

What do you guys think ?

issue_title: Add line width in graph function to customize the graph (network) edge weight
issue_body:
When creating graph (network), sometimes we need to customize the edge width. The edge width stands for how many times two nodes co-occurrence. For example, node A and B co-occur 100 times and node B and C co-occur 500 times, then the edge (link) width between B and C is five times thicker than A and B. 

However, currently in the graph function (according to the API document), there is no width options.In links, the value option is for edge length but not width.

issue_title: Zeromq hangs if connecting to invalid IPC socket
issue_body:
import zmq
    
    INVALID_ADDR = 'ipc:///tmp/idontexist.socket'
    
    context = zmq.Context()
    socket = context.socket(zmq.REQ)
    
    socket.connect(INVALID_ADDR)
    socket.send('hello')
    
    poller = zmq.Poller()
    poller.register(socket, zmq.POLLIN)
    conn = dict(poller.poll(1000))
    if conn:
        if conn.get(socket) == zmq.POLLIN:
            print "got result: ", socket.recv(zmq.NOBLOCK)
    else:
        print 'got no result'
        print 'now, zeromq hangs, press CTRL_C to finish script :('
